title: The Unmapped Settlement Above

features.name: overgrown-pathway-network
features.desc-on-touch: Roads that predate the archive, worn into the earth so deeply that time cannot erase them.

features.name: structure-footprints
features.desc-on-touch: Where buildings once stood, now only depressions in the ground. The archive was built to remember these, and still they fade.

features.name: settlement-perimeter
features.desc-on-touch: The boundary line where the old town ended and wilderness began. It is still maintained by no one, still observed by the land.

features.name: artifact-concentration-point
features.desc-on-touch: Where scattered broken things gather — pottery, metal, tools. The earth surfaces its dead slowly.

features.name: memory-keeper-tree
features.desc-on-touch: An old eucalyptus growing in what was the center square. Its rings hold dates that predate written records.

inscription: the archive came here to write down what the settlers had already written in their roads and walls. it found that the land had rewritten everything.

file.name: Settlement_Map_Comparison_Survey.txt
file.note: Leif's field survey from 2019 compared to aerial photographs from 1987, compared to local memory from 1962. Each layer contradicts the others slightly.

exits.label: back downstream to when the settlement was still remembered
where the settlement extends back further, into pre-archive time
where the old pathways lead to the source, the river's origin
back to the ruins where erosion is still visible
